current_best_seed_route: Use AIID and OECD for bounded same-event comparator pilots; keep AVID and MITRE ATLAS as secondary case-context/security overlays rather than primary incident census feeds.
publication_use_now: Table or heatmap row showing which external sources can support concrete case-level comparisons versus only partial case context.
interpretation_boundary: Capability presence does not equal broad import rights, exact same-case identity, or replicated row-level overlap.

current_best_seed_route: Use MIT AI Risk and CSET as bounded concept/harm overlays; use OECD, AIID, AVID, and MITRE ATLAS only where hazard language is attached to concrete incidents or security records.
publication_use_now: Methods/related-work row separating harm/risk concept coverage from incident-level or vulnerability-level case evidence.
interpretation_boundary: Hazard vocabulary support should not be narrated as case completeness or as permission to mirror concept-source text.

current_best_seed_route: AVID remains the primary permissive structured vulnerability import route; MITRE ATLAS adds partial vulnerability/security context but not a direct vulnerability-record substitute.
publication_use_now: Security interoperability table row showing why AVID is the first direct seed-import source and ATLAS the second graph overlay route.
interpretation_boundary: Do not treat ATLAS technique/case-study context as equivalent to AVID vulnerability IDs or as proof of realized exploitation.

current_best_seed_route: All six sources contribute taxonomy structure, but reuse posture differs sharply: AVID and MITRE ATLAS are the cleanest structured routes; AIID/OECD are bounded comparator taxonomies; MIT AI Risk and CSET remain concept-layer overlays.
publication_use_now: High-confidence heatmap row for cross-source classification coverage and dashboard legend design.
interpretation_boundary: Shared taxonomy availability does not imply shared granularity, interoperable IDs, or machine-ingest permission.

current_best_seed_route: Use OECD and MIT AI Risk for bounded policy/risk-definition framing; keep AVID and CSET as partial bridges where problem types or harm concepts overlap.
publication_use_now: Methods note row separating definition-layer support from case-level or technique-level interoperability.
interpretation_boundary: Risk-definition coverage is interpretive support only unless tied to a row-level case, source, or framework anchor.

current_best_seed_route: MITRE ATLAS is the primary technique/mitigation graph route; AVID provides secondary security technique and metric hints where directly linked to vulnerability/report records.
publication_use_now: Security-governance bridge row for graph-ready technique coverage and MITRE/AVID role separation.
interpretation_boundary: Technique coverage alone does not establish direct same-case identity, incident frequency, or normative severity.
